title: 10 best herbal creams for wheezing

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with wheezing because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated airways and reduce congestion. The cream's active ingredients, such as ursolic acid and rosmarinic acid, have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body, thereby decreasing bronchial constriction and wheezing symptoms. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to calm dry, irritated airways, further alleviating wheezing episodes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal purple coneflower creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamic acid and rosmarinic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, alleviating wheezing symptoms.

Antihistamine properties: Purple coneflower cream contains antihistamine compounds that can help block the action of histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions that can cause bronchospasm and wheezing.

Expectorant properties: The herbal extract in purple coneflower creams also has expectorant properties, which means it can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain compounds that help to soothe and calm irritated airways, providing relief from wheezing.
Seeds: The seeds are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and congestion associated with wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves according to your recipe requirements.
Combine the harvested herbs with coconut oil in a double boiler setup at a temperature of 160-170°f for 30 minutes.
Strain the herbal mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids after 10 minutes.
Add beeswax and shea butter to the strained herbal oil and melt it in a double boiler setup at a temperature of 180-190°f for 15 minutes.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized tubes or jars and allow it to cool completely at room temperature before refrigerating or storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with wheezing because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This property can help to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reducing inflammation in the airways that can cause wheezing. Additionally, licorice creams often include other herbs such as marshmallow root and slippery elm, which can help to thin mucus and make it easier to expel, further alleviating wheezing symptoms. Overall, herbal licorice creams can provide natural relief from wheezing and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic effects that can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate wheezing symptoms.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in licorice creams have antioxidant properties that can help protect the respiratory system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to wheezing.
Saponins: Saponins in licorice creams have natural expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs, making it effective for relieving wheezing.
Rhyzomes: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe bronchial passages and ease respiratory distress associated with wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Measure 200g of raw beeswax and 100g of coconut oil, then heat in a double boiler until melted.
Combine 10g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and 5ml of distilled water in a small saucepan to create an infusion.
Steep the infusion mixture for 30 minutes, then strain through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids.
Mix 50ml of the infused liquid with 100g of cooled melted beeswax and coconut oil blend until well combined and smooth.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized containers and allow to cool and set at room temperature for at least 60 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with wheezing because of their natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated airways. The herbal extract contains mucilages that help to form a protective barrier on the respiratory tract's surface, reducing inflammation and swelling. This allows for easier breathing and reduces the frequency and severity of wheezing episodes. Additionally, marshmallow creams' expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, further relieving congestion and promoting healthy lung function.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: The high mucilage content in marshmallow root helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation in the airways that can contribute to wheezing.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Marshmallow herbal creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation and swelling in the lungs and airways, which can alleviate symptoms of wheezing.

Soften and lubricate mucus: The mucilage content in marshmallow root also helps to soften and lubricate excess mucus, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion, which can contribute to wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated airways, making them an effective ingredient in creams for wheezing relief.
Barks: The barks of the marshmallow plant contain mucilages, which are natural emollients that help to moisturize and protect the respiratory tract, providing relief from wheezing and coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis flowers in full bloom and dry them immediately using desiccant or oven at low temperature.
Grind dried althaea officinalis flowers into fine powder using a spice grinder and sift through a 40-mesh screen.
Combine powdered althaea officinalis with coconut oil and beeswax in a heat-proof bowl and gently warm it in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture until it reaches a consistent texture and consistency, adding vitamin e oil and aloe vera gel if desired.
Pour the cooled and thickened mixture into sterilized containers and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or freeze for longer-term preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with wheezing because they contain a natural compound called eucalyptol, which has potent decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the chest or back, this cream can help relieve bronchial congestion by loosening mucus and reducing swelling in the airways. This allows for easier breathing and reduces the frequency and severity of wheezing episodes, making it a natural and effective remedy for respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of wheezing.

Antispasmodic properties: The essential oils present in Tasmanian Blue Gum, such as eucalyptol and cineole, have antispasmodic effects, which can help relax bronchial muscles and relieve spasms that contribute to wheezing.

Mucolytic properties: The saponins and terpenoids found in Tasmanian Blue Gum have mucolytic properties, which can help break down excess mucus and reduce its viscosity, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion and wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe respiratory issues and reduce wheezing symptoms.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to have bronchodilatory effects, helping to ease breathing difficulties associated with wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the leaves in a warm place for at least 72 hours to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Grind 50g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 100ml of coconut oil with 20g of powdered eucalyptus globulus and heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before pouring it into sterilized jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with wheezing because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to relax the airway muscles and reduce inflammation in the lungs, allowing for easier breathing. This relief from constriction and swelling enables individuals to breathe more freely and reduces the frequency and severity of wheezing episodes. Additionally, thyme's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, further alleviating respiratory issues associated with wheezing.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Thyme's antispasmodic properties can help relax and calm down airway spasms, reducing wheezing and making breathing easier.
Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds can reduce swelling and inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to wheezing by narrowing airways.
Expectorant property: Thyme's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and congestion associated with wheezing.
Seeds: Thyme seeds contain expectorant compounds that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus vulgaris leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Dry the thymus vulgaris using air circulation or oven drying method.
Grind the dried thymus vulgaris into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground thymus vulgaris with carrier cream base and beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler and stir until melted and smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with wheezing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the airways and reduce inflammation that can cause wheezing. The cream's calming effects also help to relax the muscles in the lungs, allowing for easier breathing and reducing spasms that can trigger wheezing. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of lavender help to combat infection and promote a healthy respiratory system, further alleviating symptoms of wheezing.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Lavender's antihistamine properties help to reduce inflammation and relieve itching, which can exacerbate wheezing symptoms caused by allergic reactions or asthma.

Sedative and anti-anxiety properties: The calming effects of lavender can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for wheezing in asthmatic individuals, leading to reduced frequency and severity of episodes.

Expectorant properties: Lavender's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion, a common symptom associated with wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion in the respiratory tract.
Buds: The buds of the English lavender plant are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to help relax airway muscles and alleviate wheezing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried lavender flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground lavender powder with 200ml of distilled water in a heat-resistant bowl and let it steep at room temperature for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter to remove the lavender solids and discard the solids after squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Combine the resulting lavender infusion with 100g of beeswax pellets, 20g of coconut oil, and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler and melt over low heat for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 65°c before whipping with an electric mixer or whisk until it reaches a thick and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed creams helps with wheezing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, such as 6-10 hydroxyl-eupolide and euparin, have been shown to relax airway muscles and reduce inflammation in the lungs, making it easier for individuals with wheezing conditions to breathe. Additionally, joe pye weed has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its ability to thin mucus and soothe irritated airways, providing relief from wheezing symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Joe Pye Weed contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and relieve wheezing symptoms.

Bronchodilatory effects: The herb has been shown to exhibit bronchodilatory properties, which means it can help relax and dilate the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing episodes.

Antioxidant properties: Joe Pye Weed's antioxidant compounds may help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to wheezing and other respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to relax bronchial muscles and reduce inflammation associated with wheezing.
Barks: Contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to soothe and calm irritated airways, reducing wheezing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried eupatorium perfoliatum flowers and 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the herbs release their fragrance and color.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it steep for 30 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.

Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 teaspoons of coconut oil to the infused oil and heat it gently over low heat until melted, then pour it into lip balm tubes or containers and let it cool to harden within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain creams helps with wheezing because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated airways. The cream's natural ingredients, such as plantain extract, chamomile, and calendula, work together to reduce swelling and ease congestion. This relief from inflammation allows the body to more easily expel mucus and other irritants, thus alleviating wheezing symptoms. By applying the cream topically to the chest or back, users can experience a reduction in wheezing episodes and improved breathing overall.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain creams contain compounds like allantoin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways, thus alleviating wheezing.
Antispasmodic: The plantain extract in these creams can relax smooth muscle spasms in the lungs and airways, reducing bronchial constriction and helping to ease wheezing symptoms.
Mucilaginous properties: Plantain cream's mucilage (thick, protective substance) helps soothe and protect irritated lung tissue, reducing inflammation and congestion that can contribute to wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing respiratory issues like wheezing.
Barks: The barks of the plantain plant contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and ease congestion, making them useful in treating wheezing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves from a healthy plant and rinse them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the plantago major leaves in a warm dry place or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Blend 100 grams of dried plantago major leaves with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 1 hour and 30 minutes to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with wheezing because they possess natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the bronchial tubes, reducing inflammation and constriction. The herb's flavonoids and volatile oils also help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, while its expectorant properties stimulate coughs to bring up excess phlegm. As a result, fennel creams can provide quick relief from wheezing caused by bronchitis, asthma, or other respiratory issues, allowing for smoother breathing and improved overall lung function.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Fennel cream's antispasmodic properties help to relax and calm the airways, reducing wheezing and coughing by easing bronchial spasms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can exacerbate wheezing and other respiratory issues.
Expectorant properties: Fennel cream's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing bronchial congestion and reducing inflammation associated with wheezing.
Seeds: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have natural expectorant properties that help loosen and remove mucus from the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Gather one ounce of dried foeniculum vulgare flowers and leaves.
Combine the herb with two ounces of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for four hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with wheezing because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and relax bronchial passages, reducing congestion and spasms. The herb angelica archangelica has been traditionally used to calm respiratory issues, including coughs and wheezing, by thinning mucus and easing its clearance from the airways. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to reduce irritation and inflammation in the lungs, allowing for easier breathing and alleviating wheezing symptoms.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Angelica's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in angelica cream can help reduce swelling and irritation in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to wheezing.
Bronchodilator properties: Angelica's bronchodilatory effects can help relax and open up constricted airways, making it easier for air to flow through and reducing wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Angelica archangelica contain volatile oils and other compounds that help soothe and calm respiratory issues, making them ideal for making creams to relieve wheezing.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes (underground stems) of the plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves then rinse them gently under cold running water.
Dry the angelica archangelica roots and leaves in a warm dark place for at least four hours or overnight.
Combine one part dried angelica archangelica roots and three parts coconut oil in a double boiler and heat.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for two hours then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken completely before applying it topically to alleviate wheezing symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with wheezing because of its natural ability to relax the airway muscles, allowing for easier breathing. The menthol in peppermint has a cooling effect on the throat and lungs, which helps to reduce inflammation and open up congested airways. Additionally, the herbal properties in peppermint cream have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which further help to ease wheezing by reducing swelling and constriction in the airways. As a result, peppermint creams can provide quick relief from wheezing and make breathing easier.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol, a key component of peppermint oil, helps to thin mucus and reduce its viscosity, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and wheezing caused by respiratory issues.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate wheezing and other breathing difficulties.
Expectorant action: The expectorant properties of peppermint oil help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing caused by excess phlegm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help soothe and calm irritated respiratory passages.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems of peppermint plants contain powerful analgesic and antispasmodic properties that can ease congestion and coughs.
remedy_recipe: Gather one ounce of fresh peppermint leaves dried or one teaspoon of dried peppermint powder.
Combine the peppermint leaves or powder with four ounces of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes allowing the menthol and other compounds to infuse.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids and let it cool.
Add three tablespoons of beeswax and one teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the cooled peppermint oil mixture whipping until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with wheezing because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated airways. The creams contain compounds like taraxasterol, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion in the lungs. Additionally, dandelion's natural expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing symptoms. By applying these creams topically, individuals can experience relief from wheezing and other respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as triterpenoids, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate congestion, making it easier to breathe.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion cream, including flavonoids and polyphenols, help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to wheezing.
Expectorant effects: Dandelion's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus and phlegm from the airways, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion and wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps soothe and calm respiratory issues like wheezing.
Buds: Containing triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce wheezing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves in early morning dew for maximum potency and quality.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 70-80°f for 3-4 hours.
Grind the dried flowers and leaves into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250mg of powdered taraxacum officinale with 10ml of coconut oil and 5ml of beeswax, heating the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool until solidified, then strain through cheesecloth to create a smooth, creamy texture and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with wheezing because they contain a potent combination of natural ingredients that soothe and calm inflamed airways. The antispasmodic properties of yarrow help to relax bronchial muscles, reducing wheezing and coughing fits. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds in yarrow cream reduce swelling in the throat and lungs, making it easier for air to pass through and relieving congestion. By applying yarrow cream topically, individuals can experience fast-acting relief from wheezing symptoms, allowing them to breathe more easily and comfortably.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes, help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, relieving wheezing symptoms by opening up air passages.
Expectorant: The expectorant properties of yarrow cream help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs and airways, reducing the severity of wheezing attacks and promoting easier breathing.
Antispasmodic: Yarrow's antispasmodic compounds relax bronchial smooth muscle, reducing constriction and spasms that can exacerbate wheezing, allowing for deeper and more efficient breathing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids, roots of yarrow help soothe and calm respiratory issues like wheezing by reducing inflammation.
Seeds: Containing expectorant compounds, seeds of yarrow stimulate mucus secretion and aid in removing excess phlegm from the lungs, relieving wheezing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest acmillefolium flowers in mid-summer and dry them thoroughly for 7-10 days.
Grind dried yarrow flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Melt 100g of beeswax in a double boiler at 180°f for 15 minutes while gently stirring.
Combine the ground yarrow powder with 50g of coconut oil and stir until well blended for 2 minutes.
Slowly add the melted beeswax to the yarrow-coconut oil mixture, whipping until smooth and creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with wheezing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated airways. The herb's essential oils, such as thujone and camphor, have a calming effect on the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and constriction of air passages. This relief from congestion and swelling allows for easier breathing and reduced wheezing sounds, providing natural and gentle relief to individuals suffering from respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Herbal sage creams contain antioxidants like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate respiratory issues like wheezing, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Anti-inflammatory: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as salvene, rosmarinic acid, and camphor, soothe bronchial tubes, reduce swelling, and ease congestion caused by wheezing, making it easier to breathe.

Expectorant: The expectorant properties of sage help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving respiratory tract congestion and making coughs more productive, which can help alleviate wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated respiratory tract tissues.
Seeds: Sage seeds contain essential oils with expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier in bulk weighing about 100 grams.
Combine the harvested leaves with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and steep for four hours under gentle heat at room temperature.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to separate the liquid from the solids discarding any remaining plant material.
Mix one teaspoon of beeswax with 20 milliliters of the infused oil and gently warm over low heat until melted then stir well to combine.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized glass jars sealing tightly and store in an airtight container for up to six months refrigerating before use if needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop creams helps with wheezing because they contain the essential oils of the hyssop plant, which has natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for the body to expel mucus and other irritants that can cause wheezing. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory effects of hyssop creams can also help to soothe and calm the airways, reducing the frequency and severity of wheezing episodes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid, help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate wheezing and other respiratory issues.

Expectorant properties: The expectorant compounds present in hyssop, including eucalyptol and borneol, help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving wheezing symptoms.

Antispasmodic properties: Hyssop's antispasmodic compounds, such as alpha-pinene and beta-pinene, help relax the bronchial muscles and reduce spasms, which can contribute to wheezing and other respiratory problems.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hyssop roots have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including wheezing, due to their antispasmodic properties.
Seeds: The seeds of hyssop contain a high amount of essential oils that help to soothe and calm the airways, reducing wheezing and coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried hyssopus officinalis leaves and flowers, ensuring quality and freshness for optimal potency.
Combine the dried herb with 8 ounces of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar, steeping it for 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil with its herbal properties.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 ounces of distilled water in a double boiler, heating the mixture until the wax melts and emulsifies smoothly.
Remove the cream from heat, allowing it to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Winterflower creams helps with wheezing because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated airways. The herbal extracts, such as Echinacea and Marshmallow, work to reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs, making it easier for individuals to breathe. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to loosen mucus and ease coughs, providing relief from wheezing and other respiratory symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy lung function, winterflower creams can offer natural and effective relief from wheezing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ingredients like chamomile and thyme have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and relieve wheezing symptoms.
Expectorant properties: Eucalyptus oil is a natural expectorant that can help loosen mucus and make it easier to cough up, providing relief from wheezing and congestion.
Soothing and calming properties: Chamomile, in particular, has a soothing and calming effect on the respiratory system, which can help reduce wheezing and promote relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, winterflower roots help soothe irritated airways and reduce inflammation associated with wheezing.
Barks: The bark contains antioxidants that help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and promote healthy breathing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bergenia crassifolia leaves and stems, carefully rinsing them with distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine one part dried bergenia crassifolia leaves with two parts coconut oil in a glass jar, allowing the mixture to steep for 6-8 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Mix 20% of the strained oil with 80% beeswax in a double boiler, heating until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f.
Remove the double boiler from heat, letting it cool slightly before pouring the cream into sterilized jars and allowing it to solidify completely at room temperature within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with wheezing because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce airway constriction, thereby alleviating wheezing symptoms. The herbal cream also has natural expectorant qualities, which aid in loosening and clearing mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier for people to breathe. Additionally, lemon verbena's calming effect on the respiratory system helps to soothe irritated tissues, further reducing wheezing episodes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lemon verbena cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid and verbascoside, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating symptoms of wheezing such as coughing and shortness of breath.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of lemon verbena cream help to protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate wheezing and other respiratory conditions.

Expectorant effects: Lemon verbena has natural expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion associated with wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon verbena roots are used due to their natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate inflammation in the airways and ease wheezing symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of the lemon verbena plant contain compounds that have been shown to relax bronchial muscles, reducing constriction and alleviating wheezing episodes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers from a trusted source in late summer or early fall.
Dry the plant material using air circulation at room temperature for seven to ten days or using a food dehydrator on low setting.
Grind 10 grams of dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or electric spice grinder.
Combine 5 grams of powdered verbena officinalis with 20 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler over low heat for twenty-five minutes.
Stir the mixture every five minutes until it reaches a temperature of 160°f to 180°f, then remove from heat and let cool before transferring to glass jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with wheezing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated airways. The herb's essential oils, such as cineole and camphor, help to reduce inflammation and relax constricted bronchial passages, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties help to protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing wheezing symptoms. By promoting healthy lung function, rosemary creams can provide relief from wheezing and other respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can exacerbate wheezing and make breathing more difficult.
Antioxidant: Rosemary's antioxidant properties help to protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic respiratory conditions like wheezing.
Expectorant: Rosemary's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm irritated airways, reducing wheezing symptoms.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including wheezing, due to their ability to thin mucus and ease congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and rinse them gently with cold water.
Dry the leaves in a dehydrator at 30°c for 3 hours or air-dry them for 24 hours.
Combine 20g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 100ml of coconut oil and heat them at 60°c for 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix the resulting oil infusion with 10% beeswax and whip it until it forms a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek creams helps with wheezing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, work together to reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating constriction and making it easier for air to flow through the lungs. Additionally, fenugreek's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, further reducing wheezing symptoms. As a result, herbal fenugreek creams can provide effective relief from wheezing and other respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fenugreek cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the lungs, which can contribute to wheezing symptoms.

Bronchodilatory properties: The saponins present in fenugreek cream have a bronchodilating effect, which can help relax airway smooth muscle, reducing constriction and opening up airways to improve breathing.
Mucolytic properties: Fenugreek cream's mucolytic properties can break down and dissolve excess mucus in the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, which help soothe and calm irritated airways, relieving wheezing symptoms.
Seeds: Containing flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing bronchial constriction and wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried fenugreek leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix 20 milliliters of sweet almond oil with the ground fenugreek powder in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 5 drops of lavender essential oil and stir well to combine, allowing the mixture to infuse for an additional 5 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool completely before transferring the mixture to a clean glass jar, which will require 30 minutes.
Store the herbal cream in the refrigerator at a temperature of 4 degrees celsius and use within 6 months or until expiration date.

The herb's essential oils, such as citral and geraniol, help to relax bronchial muscles, reducing spasms and constriction that can cause wheezing. Additionally, lemon balm's antimicrobial properties help to combat infections that may contribute to respiratory issues. remedy_properties: Antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects: Lemon balm cream helps reduce inflammation and allergic reactions in the airways, which can alleviate symptoms of wheezing.

Sedative and relaxing properties: The calming effects of lemon balm can help to relax bronchial smooth muscle, making it easier to breathe and reducing the frequency and severity of wheezing episodes.

Antiviral properties: Lemon balm has been shown to have antiviral activity against respiratory viruses such as the common cold and flu, which can contribute to wheezing by infecting and inflaming the airways.

Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated airways, providing relief from wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves from a trusted supplier or your own garden in the morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 12 hours or by air-drying them for 3-4 days.
Combine 20g of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container and discard the solids after 14 days of infusion to produce a strong herbal extract.
Mix the herbal extract with 200ml of cooled down beeswax and 100ml of vitamin e oil in a double boiler while gently stirring until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for wheezing are topical preparations that combine natural ingredients with soothing properties to alleviate respiratory issues, particularly wheezing. These creams help by reducing inflammation and congestion in the airways, allowing for easier breathing and alleviating coughs. Examples of herbal creams that provide relief include those containing eucalyptus, peppermint, and lavender oils, which can be applied directly to the chest or back. By providing instant relief, these creams can greatly improve the quality of life for individuals with wheezing, allowing them to sleep more comfortably and participate in daily activities without discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat wheezing with herbal creams is by applying a topical cream containing eucalyptus oil, which helps to loosen mucus and reduce congestion. A combination of eucalyptus, peppermint, and thyme oils in a base cream can also provide relief from wheezing symptoms. Apply the cream topically to the chest and back to help ease breathing and reduce coughing fits.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to wheezing that are treated with herbal creams are bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). These conditions involve inflammation and constriction of the airways, leading to coughing, shortness of breath, and wheezing. Herbal creams containing ingredients like eucalyptus, peppermint, and ginger have natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that can help alleviate these symptoms by soothing the respiratory tract and loosening mucus.
